response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said brings 16+ years of software development experience with genuine polyglot breadth and some distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second. However, the Principal Growth/Experimentation role requires deep, demonstrable expertise in A/B testing infrastructure, feature flagging, rollout-risk management, telemetry pipelines, and statistical analysis — none of which appear meaningfully in the resume. The candidate's career has been primarily product-layer and application-level full-stack development, without the growth measurement platforms, experiment assignment systems, or cross-team experimentation ownership that defines this IC6 scope. The misalignment on core competencies is too fundamental to overcome at this seniority level.
strengths: Genuine polyglot depth across TypeScript, C/C++, Java, DLang, and Node.js with both web and systems-level production experience
Distributed micro-services exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O and high-performance data pipelines
Current AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP frameworks, agentic tooling) partially addresses the preferred AI-tooling acceleration criterion
concerns: Zero demonstrated experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging systems, or rollout-risk management — the absolute core of this role
No evidence of telemetry or metrics infrastructure ownership, statistical methods, bias detection, or data-driven product decision-making at platform scale

Career trajectory (Senior/Staff on product features across multiple companies) falls well short of Principal/IC6 expectations around owning multi-team growth strategy and cross-surface measurement platforms

key_factors.factor: No experimentation or metrics platform experience
Seniority and scope gap vs. IC6
Distributed systems and high-throughput service exposure
